_comment: Phase-4 real-device validation descriptor for the network-provisioning pipeline. The Harvester FETCHES this from its raw GitHub URL (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/paichart/paichart/main/descriptors/ceos-lab-readonly-descriptor.json) — put that URL in the pipeline task description. It points at sydasif/nornir-mcp-server fronting a 2-node Arista cEOS lab on prod, reachable at the STABLE named cloudflared tunnel ceos-lab.paichart.app (systemd service on prod → localhost:3107). Declares the READ-ONLY harvest subset ONLY: list_devices + fetch_data(NAPALM getters). The service ALSO physically exposes Netmiko show_commands (free-text) + apply_config (mutating) — deliberately NOT declared, so the pipeline never sees or calls them (descriptor-level restriction, not service-level R1 enforcement). HONEST CAVEAT: nornir-mcp authenticates to the device with STATIC creds, NOT the JWKS-forwarded identity WS4 R2a mandates — so this is NOT WS4-conformant. It validates the COGNITION pipeline (harvest -> design -> change-package vs REAL EOS) + R9-at-the-wire, not the identity contract. Rig + runbook: copov15 .claude/knowledge/pipelines/network-provisioning/phase4-ceos-rig/.
name: ceos-lab-readonly

tools.name: list_devices
tools.description: List network devices and inventory (devices, groups, or both). Read-only. Call this first to discover device names before fetch_data.

details.description: Return full device attributes (for a devices query)

tools.name: fetch_data
tools.description: Execute one or more READ-ONLY NAPALM getters against the EOS devices and return structured state (running-config, interfaces, facts, BGP neighbors, etc.). This is the Phase-0 harvest surface. Read-only by construction — NAPALM getters never mutate device state.

getters.description: NAPALM getter names to run (short form, no 'get_' prefix). All entries are read-only EOS getters.

filter_name.description: Restrict to one inventory device name (e.g. 'ceos1')
